history.content:
<thought>
I can see walls (#) directly to the right and open floor (.) to my left/front-left, while lava (L) is on the far right side of the view. To avoid lava, I will turn left so I can move toward the open area rather than toward the lava region.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
In my view, lava (L) is immediately to my left (the cell with L). The cell in front appears to be wall (#), so moving forward is blocked. To avoid the left lava while not hitting the wall, I will turn right and look for a free floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The lava region is to my right in this view, while the cell in front is a wall (#), so I can’t move forward. I should turn left to face a safer direction away from lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
This matches an earlier situation: I can see lava directly to my left, and the cell in front is a wall (#), so MOVE_FORWARD is unsafe/impossible. I will turn right to face away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
In front of me is a floor cell (.) and not lava, and moving forward keeps me away from the lava clusters visible to the right/side. I should advance toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
